title: Dream Andaman
description: The Andaman and Nicobar Islands is a union territory of India comprising 836 islands, of which only 31 are inhabited. The islands are grouped into two main clusters: the northern Andaman Islands and the southern Nicobar Islands, separated by the 150 km-wide Ten Degree Channel.

activities.title: Private transfer from Airport to Hotel
Cellular Jail
activities.description:
Cellular Jail, located in Port Blair, Andaman and Nicobar Islands, is a historic colonial prison built by the British in 1906. It is also known as “Kala Pani”.

🔹 Why It Was Built

The British used it to imprison Indian freedom fighters who opposed colonial rule. Prisoners were kept in extreme isolation to break their spirit.

🔹 Structure

7 wings (originally)

693 solitary cells

Designed so prisoners could not see or communicate with each other

🔹 Historical Importance

Many famous freedom fighters were imprisoned here, including:

Vinayak Damodar Savarkar

Today, it is a national memorial and a symbol of India’s struggle for independence. A light and sound show is conducted in the evening to narrate its history.

It stands as a reminder of the sacrifices made for India’s freedom 🇮🇳

activities.title: Ferry Ride from Port Blair to Havelock- Shared Transfers (MAK)
activities.description: Take in the beautiful views as you take a Port Blair to Havelock Island ferry with your partner or family. Once on-board, you will be welcomes with tea/coffee for a refreshing experience. Feel the wind in your hair and grab beautiful views en route! Note: This is a shared ferry transfer service. In case of any delays, that cause the guests to miss the ferry, no refunds will be provided. Ferry tickets and timings are subject to availability. Govt. ferry would be provided based on the availability and operation. The ferry will be either Makruzz / Nautika Lite / Nautika 1

activities.title: Radhanagar Beach
activities.description: Radhanagar Beach (Beach No. 7) on Havelock Island (Swaraj Dweep), Andaman, is a renowned, pristine, Blue Flag-certified destination famous for its white sand, turquoise waters, and stunning sunsets. Often ranked among Asia's best, it offers calm, shallow waters ideal for swimming and, in specific zones, snorkeling and scuba diving
